channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Building a Voice-Controlled Local AI Agent with LangGraph and Mem0
item.description: I wanted to work with Local AI Agents; it turned to be one of the more fun projects I've worked on, and a valuable primer on TUI UI/UX. Why I Built This The goal was simple enough on paper: build...

item.title: Week 16 Dev Log: From First Customer to 56 MCP Tools — Building an AI-Native Website Publisher
item.description: I'm building WebsitePublisher.ai — a platform where AI assistants build and publish websites through MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools. This week: first paying customer, team collaboration, and...

item.title: We built a firewall for LLM apps
item.description: Web apps have WAFs. APIs have rate limiters and auth layers. LLM apps? Most of them have nothing between the user and the model. If you're shipping LLM features, you've got a new attack surface —...

item.title: Terraform State Locking: A Guide for Growing Teams
item.description: Prevent state file corruption and race conditions in your collaborative projects. Originally published on devopsstart.com, this guide walks through implementing Terraform state locking using AWS,...

item.title: 25 Workflow Automation and Process Agent Patterns on AWS You Can Steal Right Now
item.description: Originally published on Build With AWS. Subscribe for weekly AWS builds. A logistics coordinator at a mid-size manufacturer spends every Monday morning copying order data from the ERP into a...

item.title: Batch Rename Photos with AI: SEO-Friendly Filenames (2026)
item.description: Why image filenames matter for SEO (and why you are probably getting it wrong) Google Image Search is one of the highest-traffic sources for photographers, e-commerce sites, and content creators....

item.title: Embedded Linux Development - Part 3
item.description: In this tutorial, I'll go with the third stage of embedded Linux development. That's the Linux kernel. It's the responsible for: Manage all the hardware resources: CPU, memory, I/O. Provide a set...

item.title: Batch Compress Images Free — No Signup, No Upload to Server (2026)
item.description: The problem with server-based image compression. When you use a tool like TinyPNG, iLoveIMG, or Compressor dot io, your images are uploaded to their servers. The compression happens on their...

item.title: How AI Image Renaming Boosts Your SEO (2026 Guide)
item.description: Why image filenames matter for SEO Google uses image filenames as one of several signals to understand what an image depicts. This is documented directly in Google's official image guidelines :...

item.title: Methodology
item.description: Alt text is no longer optional. It is a ranking factor, an accessibility requirement, and increasingly generated by AI. But how accurate are these AI-generated descriptions really? I tested three...

item.title: SwitchBot’s button-pressing robot is now available with a rechargeable battery
item.description: SwitchBot is introducing a new version of the tiny button-pressing robot that helped launch the company a decade ago. The functionality of the new SwitchBot Bot is exactly the same as the original...

item.title: Can Puck reinvent the news business for the influencer age?
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Sarah Personette, CEO of Puck, which is a fancy new media company that’s been around for just about five years now. Puck hires big stars and gives them newsletters that are...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly building an AI clone to replace him in meetings
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg could soon have an AI clone of himself to interact with and provide feedback to employees, according to a report from the Financial Times. Sources tell the outlet that...

item.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
item.description: If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. The 2026 AI Index from Stanford University’s...

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz blockade is causing a slow-moving food crisis
item.description: Farmers are very busy in the spring, under pressure to get crops into the ground just as the Northern Hemisphere begins to thaw. But this year has been different for many, thanks in large part to...

item.title: Roblox will need age verification to make sure you’re at least 9 years old
item.description: Roblox is about to make a big change to how you can access games on its platform: If you want to play any game that's intended for users nine or older, you will have to complete an age...

item.title: Roblox has games again
item.description: Roblox is making a subtle but notable change. After swapping the word "game" for "experience" all over its website and mobile apps in 2021 while the Epic Games v. Apple trial was taking place,...

item.title: The Download: how humans make decisions, and Moderna’s “vaccine” word games
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. You have no choice in reading this article—maybe How do...

item.title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
item.description: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi...

item.title: The Internet's Most Powerful Archiving Tool Is in Peril
item.description: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.

item.title: Did Neuralink make the wrong bet?
item.description: Elon Musk promised Neuralink would bring superhuman abilities and minds merged with AI. Then he fueled a runaway hype train for his brain implant technology, which ended up with a grisly record...

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox Gamepad Cursor adds a virtual mouse to handhelds
item.description: Microsoft has started testing its own virtual mouse cursor inside its Xbox mode for Windows-based handhelds. The new Gamepad Cursor option provides a quick way to turn the left stick of a handheld...

item.title: AI Chatbots and Trust
item.description: All the leading AI chatbots are sycophantic, and that’s a problem: Participants rated sycophantic AI responses as more trustworthy than balanced ones. They also said they were more likely to come...

item.title: Job titles of the future: Wildlife first responder
item.description: Grizzly bears have made such a comeback across eastern Montana that in 2017, the state hired its first-ever prairie-based grizzly manager: wildlife biologist Wesley Sarmento. For some seven years,...

item.title: You have no choice in reading this article—maybe
item.description: Uri Maoz loved doing his human research, back when he was getting his PhD. He was studying a very specific topic in computational neuroscience: how the brain instructs our arms to move and how our...

item.title: AI Agents Are Coming for Your Dating Life
item.description: The developers of Pixel Societies are using AI agents to simulate social interactions. It's an attempt optimize the process of choosing new colleagues, friends, and even romantic partners.

item.title: Sam Altman reportedly targeted in second attack
item.description: Sam Altman was seemingly targeted by a second attack on Sunday morning, with two suspects arrested following a shooting at his Russian Hill residence, The San Francisco Standard reports. The...

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
item.description: The North Korean hacking group tracked as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and...

item.title: Huawei beats Apple and Samsung with new wide foldable
item.description: Apple and Samsung have both been strongly linked with plans for foldable phones in a boxy, wide aspect ratio, but it looks like another company will get there first. Huawei has just revealed the...

item.title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
item.description: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.
